organization.name: Finaxis
organization.description: A regional financial firm with compliance-driven security investment but underdeveloped recovery capabilities

asset_management.notes: Asset inventory is partially automated. Hardware tracked via network scans; software inventory relies on manual spreadsheets updated quarterly. Data classification schema defined but inconsistently applied.

business_environment.notes: Core business processes documented but dependency mapping incomplete. No formal supply chain risk assessment program; vendor reviews conducted ad hoc.

governance.roles_responsibilities: Partially defined — gaps exist in cloud and third-party coverage
governance.notes: 8-person security team. Security briefed to CTO quarterly; no standing board security committee. Policies reviewed annually but some are 18+ months out of date.

risk_assessment.notes: Annual third-party risk assessment supplemented by quarterly internal scans. No threat intelligence subscriptions. Penetration test conducted 14 months ago.

risk_management_strategy.notes: Risk register maintained but owner assignments inconsistent. Remediation timelines often missed with no escalation path.

access_control.notes: MFA enforced for corporate SSO but contractor and third-party access relies on username/password. Shared service accounts exist for legacy systems. No PAM tooling deployed.

awareness_training.notes: Annual mandatory training module. Phishing simulations run quarterly but results are not consistently tied to remedial training. No role-based programs for high-risk users.

data_security.notes: Encryption enforced on production databases and file transfers. No DLP tooling deployed. PII handling relies on policy controls rather than technical enforcement.

information_protection.notes: CIS Level 1 hardening applied to new builds. Legacy systems carry unmitigated deviations. Change management process exists but emergency changes frequently bypass review.

maintenance.notes: Critical patches targeted within 30 days; actual compliance around 78%. Several legacy financial systems cannot be patched without vendor coordination, creating extended exposure windows.

protective_technology.endpoint_protection: Traditional AV with cloud-based reputation lookup
protective_technology.email_security: Standard filtering with spam and basic phishing detection
protective_technology.notes: Network segmented between core zones but no microsegmentation within segments. Traditional AV on endpoints; no behavioral EDR. No WAF protecting customer-facing applications.

anomalies_and_events.notes: SIEM performs basic log correlation but no formal behavioral baseline has been established. High false-positive rate results in alert fatigue. No UEBA capabilities.

security_monitoring.notes: SIEM covers core financial systems and perimeter but gaps exist in cloud workloads and third-party SaaS. No after-hours monitoring coverage; alerts queue until next business day. Log retention at 90 days does not meet recommended 12-month guidance.

detection_processes.mttd: ~24 hours for critical threats (business hours); up to 72 hours for after-hours events
detection_processes.notes: IDS/IPS at perimeter. No EDR or NDR deployed. No proactive threat hunting capability. Detection relies entirely on reactive SIEM alerting.

response_planning.notes: IR plan documented and reviewed annually. Playbooks exist for ransomware and data breach scenarios but are not regularly tested. No tabletop exercises conducted in 18+ months.

communications.notes: Internal escalation paths defined. No pre-drafted external communications or PR crisis runbook. Regulatory notification contacts documented (OCC, FFIEC) but workflows not rehearsed.

analysis.notes: No in-house forensics capability or tooling. Post-incident root cause analysis performed for major events. No IR retainer in place — incident response vendors would need to be engaged ad hoc.

mitigation.notes: Manual containment procedures documented. No SOAR or automated response capability. Network isolation requires manual coordination between security and IT ops.

improvements.notes: Post-incident reviews held for major events but improvement items are tracked informally and completion is inconsistent.

recovery_planning.dr_site: Warm standby — failover procedures documented but not validated
recovery_planning.notes: DR plan drafted and approved two years ago. Never been exercised or tested in any form. RTO/RPO commitments are aspirational with no evidence base. No formal Business Continuity Plan exists — only the DR plan document. DR site configurations may have drifted from production.

improvements.notes: No DR testing has been conducted. Recovery testing has been deferred annually due to budget and scheduling constraints. There is no established cadence or ownership for recovery validation.

communications.notes: No stakeholder communication plan for recovery scenarios. Executive notification procedure exists in the DR document but has never been rehearsed. Customer communication in a major outage would be improvised.

backups.backup_frequency: Weekly full, daily incremental for core systems
backups.notes: Backups are being performed but restore success has not been verified in 18+ months. Offsite replication to a secondary data center exists but is not air-gapped. No immutable backup copies. Backup encryption enabled but key management is informal. In a ransomware scenario, backup integrity cannot be guaranteed.

recent_incidents.impact: Low — finance employee flagged suspicious wire request before transfer
recent_incidents.detection_time: Human detection, ~6 hours after initial email
recent_incidents.notes: Wire fraud attempt via spoofed executive email. Caught by employee due to awareness training. No technical controls flagged the email.

recent_incidents.impact: Medium — 47 customer accounts accessed before detection
recent_incidents.detection_time: ~28 hours (detected during business hours review of prior night logs)
recent_incidents.notes: Attack occurred overnight. Not detected until morning log review. Affected accounts locked and customers notified per regulatory requirements. Remediation took 3 days.

compliance.audits: Annual third-party audit for PCI DSS; internal audits semi-annual
compliance.audit_findings: 3 moderate findings (1 remediated, 2 in progress), 5 low findings

budget.notes: Security budget has remained flat for three years. No cyber insurance policy — procurement was initiated but stalled due to premium cost concerns. Recovery and resilience investments consistently deprioritized in favor of compliance-mandated controls.
